descriptions.description:
The screen displays a Wikipedia article titled "Parallax Scrolling" open in a web browser. The article discusses the history and techniques of parallax scrolling, with an image showing a 2D scene demonstrating the effect. The article is marked as needing additional citations and contains sections on methods like layer method, sprite method, repeating pattern/animation method, and raster method.  A "Video game graphics" sidebar is visible on the right side of the screen, listing types and topics related to the subject. No code editor or active coding environment is visible; it appears to be a static informational display.

```
(No code visible)
```

The visual output is the Wikipedia article itself, with accompanying image of a 2D scene depicting parallax scrolling. The image shows buildings and sky with varying layers to illustrate the effect.

The screen displays a Wikipedia page titled "Parallax scrolling" with sections on methods and examples. The right side of the screen features a settings panel with options for "Layer method," "Sprite method," "Repeating pattern/animation method," and "Raster method" each with radio buttons for Small, Standard, Large, Wide, Automatic, Light and Dark. A visual example shows three layers: a cloud layer, vegetation layer, and ground layer with an animation preview in the middle. The example references a web design blog post by Glutinix from 2007 and SXSW Interactive 2008.

```
Cloud layer - back Vegetation layer - middle Ground layer - front Animation
```

The UI appears to be a web browser displaying the Wikipedia article, with an overlaid settings panel likely part of an interactive demonstration or tutorial. No code editor is visible in the current screenshot.
